sprint: VihuPlanet Magic Card Redesign -- Companion-Led Front, Ornate Frame, Guardian Panel
task: A full visual redesign of js/magicCardArt.js's two-sided card art, driven by a detailed user-supplied reference mockup. The reference introduced two genuinely new data concepts (Level/XP, Coins) that don't exist anywhere in the platform -- confirmed with the user before building: Level/Coins render as disclosed, explicit static placeholders (never derived from real data), and 'SERIAL#' is simply the existing, already-correct card.recallCode relabelled to match the reference's vocabulary, with honest 'Type this to come home' copy rather than a fabricated register mechanic. The front's hierarchy now leads with the bonded Story Companion (large portrait window, name, species) per Companion Canon V2's own 'the Magic Card is the permanent record of the Creator Bond' framing, with the Creator's own identity in a new Creator Info panel below (nickname, disclosed Level/Coin placeholders, member-since), real Stories/Worlds counts, and a new Guardian (Lumo) panel present on both faces regardless of companion-bonding status, using Lumo's own real, already-uploaded portrait art. A companion-less legacy card degrades gracefully to its prior nickname-headline layout. Three new shared helpers (_drawCardFrame, _panel, _portraitCircle) keep front and back visually consistent. The back gained a real header/subtitle and a SERIAL#/Guardian two-panel row beneath the unchanged constellation grid. Verified via real Playwright screenshots of all three states (companion-bonded, reveal-gated back, companion-less fallback) matching the reference, plus full regression across the Companion/Magic-Card/Creator suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change; confined to js/magicCardArt.js and js/magicCardUI.js.
